source_brd.notes: Source BRD preserved as received from the upstream business team.

normalized_brd_requirements.source_text: The outage management capability shall remain available during regional infrastructure failures.
normalized_brd_requirements.normalized_text: The outage management solution must maintain service continuity during regional infrastructure failure scenarios.
normalized_brd_requirements.normalization_rationale: Original BRD requirement is explicit and only needed structural normalization for downstream traceability.

normalized_brd_requirements.source_text: Operators must be able to continue dispatch activities during major outage events without loss of situational awareness.
normalized_brd_requirements.normalized_text: The solution must support continued operator dispatch workflows during major outage events with preserved operational visibility.
normalized_brd_requirements.normalization_rationale: The source BRD expressed the need as narrative business prose without a formal requirement identifier, so a surrogate normalization ID was assigned in the companion artifact only.

technical_requirements.requirement_statement: The outage management platform shall fail over to a secondary region within 60 seconds during a primary region outage.

traceability.brd_requirement_text: The outage management capability shall remain available during regional infrastructure failures.
traceability.trace_rationale: Regional failover is a necessary technical mechanism to satisfy the BRD continuity requirement during regional failure conditions.

technical_requirements.environment: Normal operations during an active outage event
technical_requirements.response: System routes processing and user access to the secondary region
technical_requirements.response_measure: Critical outage workflows are restored within 60 seconds

verification_approach.activity: Execute controlled regional failover testing to confirm continuity thresholds are met under outage conditions.

validation_approach.activity: Run outage response drills with system operators to validate that operational continuity expectations are met during a regional failover event.

technical_requirements.requirement_statement: The outage management platform shall present operator status, crew assignment, and network state changes within 2 seconds of source system updates during active dispatch operations.

traceability.brd_requirement_text: The solution must support continued operator dispatch workflows during major outage events with preserved operational visibility.
traceability.trace_rationale: Timely status propagation is required to preserve operational visibility during dispatch workflows.

technical_requirements.stimulus: High-volume dispatch and telemetry updates during a major outage
technical_requirements.response: The platform refreshes operational views with near-real-time updates
technical_requirements.response_measure: Critical operational data is displayed within 2 seconds of source updates

verification_approach.activity: Run automated performance tests against dispatch update flows to verify end-to-end latency remains within the defined threshold.

validation_approach.activity: Validate with dispatch operators that the update latency supports real-time decision-making during simulated outage events.

traceability_gaps.candidate_technical_requirement: The outage management platform shall retain operational audit logs for seven years.
traceability_gaps.why_it_appears_necessary: The platform supports regulated utility operations where audit retention is typically required for compliance and post-incident review.
traceability_gaps.missing_brd_requirement: The BRD does not include an explicit retention requirement describing audit scope, duration, or compliance basis.
traceability_gaps.recommended_action: Request an upstream BRD clarification or addendum that explicitly states the retention obligation before accepting this technical requirement into the baseline.
